explanation: The property states that globally, if the system is currently in PCV mode AND ventilation is active AND the inspiratory phase ends AND the command to switch to PSV mode has been received from the GUI, then in the very next state, the system shall transition to PSV mode. This directly captures the conditions under which the transition from PCV to PSV must occur.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in PCV mode and ventilating, and simultaneously the inspiratory phase ends and the command to change to PSV mode is received from the GUI, then in the very next state, the system shall transition to PSV mode. This directly captures the required transition logic under the specified conditions.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in PCV mode, ventilation is active, the inspiratory phase is ending, and the command to switch to PSV mode has been received from the GUI, then in the very next state, the system must transition into PSV mode. This captures the requirement that the transition from PCV to PSV should occur at the specified conditions.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PCV mode (`PCVMode`) AND ventilation is active (`ventilating`) AND the inspiratory phase ends (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`) AND a command to switch to PSV mode has been received from the GUI (`PSVModeSelected`), then in the very next state (X), the system will transition to PSV mode (`PSVMode`). This captures that the transition must occur when all specified conditions are met.

The property specifies that globally, if the system is currently operating in 'PCVMode' and is actively 'ventilating', and a 'PSVModeSelected' command has been received from the GUI, and the 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' is detected (marking the end of the PCV inspiratory time), then in the very next state, the system shall transition to 'PSVMode'. This precisely captures the conditions and the immediate timing of the mode change as stated in the natural language.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV Mode, ventilation is active, the PSV mode change command is received from the GUI, and the inspiratory phase ends (indicating the end of the PCV inspiratory time), then in the very next state, the system shall transition to PSV Mode. This captures the requirement that the transition from PCV to PSV occurs at the end of the inspiratory time if the necessary conditions (ventilation on, command received) are met while in PCV mode.

The natural language describes a response property where a transition to PCV mode occurs under specific conditions. The conditions for this transition are that ventilation is active ('ventilating'), the system is currently in PCV mode ('PCVMode'), the inspiratory phase is ending ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), and the change mode command to PSV has been received from the GUI ('PSVModeSelected'). These combined conditions form the antecedent of the implication (bool_exp1). The consequence is that the system enters PSV mode ('PSVMode'). The phrase 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time' suggests an immediate transition in the next state following the conditions being met, which is captured by the 'X' (next) operator. Therefore, template ID 31, which is G (bool_exp1 -> (X bool_exp3)), is chosen to represent that globally, if the conditions in bool_exp1 are met, then in the next state, bool_exp3 will be true.

The property states that if the system is in PCV mode, ventilation is active, the inspiratory phase ends, and the command to switch to PSV mode is received from the GUI, then the system shall eventually transition to PSV mode. This is a common response pattern, where a set of conditions (antecedent) leads to an eventual desired state (consequent). The global operator (G) ensures this holds true at all times. The future operator (F) indicates that the transition to PSV mode will eventually occur after the conditions are met.

The LTL property describes a responsive behavior: whenever a specific set of conditions (bool_exp1) becomes true, the system must transition to a new mode (bool_exp3) in the very next state. The phrase "The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur" indicates that a new state (PSV mode) is entered immediately after the conditions are met. Template ID 60 is chosen as it accurately captures this 'onset' or 'becomes true' trigger for an immediate (Next) consequence. bool_exp1 combines all the trigger conditions: 'ventilation is on' (ventilating), 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time' (inspiratoryPhaseEnd and PCVMode), and 'change mode command has been received from the GUI' (PSVModeSelected). bool_exp3 represents the new state, 'PSV Mode'.

The property states that globally, if ventilation is active OR the inspiratory phase ends and a PSV mode selection command is received, then in the next state, the system transitions to PSV mode. This covers both conditions for the transition as specified in the natural language.
